mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity sell-offs over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Joint decline periods yield positive 60-day returns in > 50% of historical cases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output that generates positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production changes fail to predict positive 5-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.01 in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation readings above 208 on the CPIAUCSL index trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Returns are positive in > 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 208.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 60-day returns in > 50% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output that supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth periods produce negative 20-day returns in > 50% of cases over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals manufacturing weakness that precedes equity market corrections, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production changes fail to predict negative 5-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in late-cycle regimes.
